text.title: Cross-Platform Token Distribution Pipeline
text.summary: Pipeline stages for distributing design tokens from a Figma/Tokens Studio source to CSS, SCSS, iOS Swift, Android XML, and React Native via Style Dictionary transforms.

rule.statement: Use Style Dictionary as the sole transform engine; never hand-edit platform output files.
rule.rationale: Hand-editing outputs breaks the pipeline: the next Style Dictionary build overwrites changes and introduces undetected drift.

rule.statement: Treat Tokens Studio JSON as the single source of truth; all platforms read from it, none write to it.
rule.rationale: Multiple sources of truth diverge silently; a single authoritative source makes drift detectable via CI diff.

section.title: Pipeline Stages
section.p: Figma Tokens (designer) → Tokens Studio export (JSON) → Style Dictionary build → per-platform outputs committed to repo.

rule.statement: Commit generated platform outputs (CSS, Swift, XML) to the repo; never resolve tokens at app runtime.
rule.rationale: Runtime resolution adds a failure point and makes token values invisible to code review and visual regression tools.

rule.statement: Use one Style Dictionary transformGroup per platform; override individual transforms only when strictly necessary.
rule.rationale: Over-customizing transforms makes the pipeline fragile and difficult to upgrade when Style Dictionary releases breaking changes.

section.title: CI Drift Check

rule.statement: Run the token build in CI on every PR and block merges when platform output files diverge from the current source.
rule.rationale: Drift is invisible without automated checking; a stale iOS Swift file ships wrong brand colors to the App Store.

section.reference: CI script that fails the build when Style Dictionary outputs are stale.
Style Dictionary config skeleton covering all five platform targets.

antipattern.description: Using rem values in iOS or dp values in CSS output.
antipattern.reason: Style Dictionary transforms are unit-and-platform-aware but trip on edge cases; always specify the unit transform explicitly per platform.

antipattern.description: Relying on Tokens Studio JSON being W3C-compatible without validation.
antipattern.reason: Tokens Studio JSON dialect drifts from the W3C draft; round-tripping breaks alias references unless validated on each export.

antipattern.description: Semantic token aliasing across platforms expecting one-level resolution.
antipattern.reason: Android XML resolves only one level deep without custom resolvers; deep alias chains silently produce wrong values.
